reasoning: The Lean encoding matches the informal statement precisely. The tuple order (p, r, q, s) matches, the conditions Nat.Prime p ∧ Nat.Prime q ∧ r > 1 ∧ s > 1 translate "p and q prime, r and s greater than 1" correctly, and the equation |(p^r : ℤ) - q^s| = 1 correctly lifts the difference to ℤ to handle the absolute value. Although the informal statement says "integer solutions" and the Lean uses ℕ, the constraints (primality forces positivity, r,s > 1 forces positivity) make the ℕ and ℤ solution sets identical, so this is not a semantic change. The "find all" encoding as set equality is the standard convention for this benchmark.
